volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Seven against Thebes (Section 2.230 - Section 390)

sections.label: Section 2.230
sections.original: ἀνδρῶν τάδ ἐστί , σφάγια καὶ χρηστήρια
sections.translation: It is the man ’ s duty to offer victims and sacrifices to the gods when they test their enemy ; your duty is to be silent and to remain inside the house .

sections.label: Section 3.233
sections.translation: By the will of the gods we inhabit an unconquered city , and the rampart withstands the enemy throng .

sections.label: Section 3.235
sections.translation: What indignation makes you resent this ?

sections.label: Section 3.236
sections.original: οὔτοι φθονῶ σοι δαιμόνων τιμᾶν γένος :
sections.translation: I do not begrudge your honor of the divine race ; but lest you make the citizens cowardly , be calm and do not be overly fearful .

sections.label: Section 3.239
sections.translation: When I heard the strange and jumbled clashes ,

sections.label: Section 3.240
sections.translation: I came in trembling fear to this citadel , our seat of worship .

sections.label: Section 3.242
sections.original: μή νυν , ἐὰν θνῄσκοντας ἢ τετρωμένους
sections.translation: If , then , you hear that men are dying or wounded , do not seize on the news with loud wailing . For this is the food of Ares , human blood .

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἀκούω γ ἱππικῶν φρυαγμάτων .
sections.translation: Oh , but I hear horses snorting !

sections.label: Section 246
sections.original: μή νυν ἀκούουσ ἐμφανῶς ἄκου ἄγαν .
sections.translation: Hear them , then , but not too clearly .

sections.label: Section 247
sections.translation: The city groans from deep in the earth , as though we are surrounded .

sections.label: Section 248
sections.original: οὐκοῦν ἔμ ἀρκεῖ τῶνδε βουλεύειν πέρι .
sections.translation: Surely it is enough that I am making plans for this ?

sections.label: Section 249
sections.original: δέδοικ , ἀραγμὸς δ ἐν πύλαις ὀφέλλεται .
sections.translation: I am terrified — the crashing at the gates is increasing .

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: οὐ σῖγα μηδὲν τῶνδ ἐρεῖς κατὰ πτόλιν ;
sections.translation: Won ’ t you be silent , and speak none of this throughout the city ?

sections.label: Section 251
sections.translation: Divine company , do not betray our fortifications !

sections.label: Section 252
sections.original: οὐκ ἐς φθόρον [*] σιγῶσ ἀνασχήσει τάδε ;
sections.translation: Damn you ! Will you not endure these events in silence ?

sections.label: Section 253
sections.original: θεοὶ πολῖται , μή με δουλείας τυχεῖν .
sections.translation: Gods of our city ! Do not let my fate be slavery !

sections.label: Section 254
sections.original: αὐτὴ σὺ δουλοῖς καὶ σὲ καὶ [*] πᾶσαν πόλιν .
sections.translation: You would enslave both me and all the city .

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: ὦ παγκρατὲς Ζεῦ , τρέψον εἰς ἐχθροὺς βέλος .
sections.translation: Almighty Zeus , turn your missile against the enemy !

sections.label: Section 256
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , γυναικῶν οἷον ὤπασας γένος .
sections.translation: O Zeus , what a breed you have made for us in women !

sections.label: Section 257
sections.original: μοχθηρόν , ὥσπερ ἄνδρας ὧν ἁλῷ πόλις .
sections.translation: A breed steeped in misery , just like men whose city is captured .

sections.label: Section 258
sections.translation: Why are your words ill-omened , when you still grasp the gods ’ statues ?

sections.label: Section 259
sections.translation: In my weakness fear controls my tongue .

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: αἰτουμένῳ μοι κοῦφον εἰ δοίης τέλος .
sections.translation: If only you would grant my plea for a small service .

sections.label: Section 261
sections.original: λέγοις ἂν ὡς τάχιστα , καὶ τάχ εἴσομαι .
sections.translation: Please state it as quickly as possible , and I will quickly know what to do .

sections.label: Section 262
sections.original: σίγησον , ὦ τάλαινα , μὴ φίλους φόβει .
sections.translation: Be silent , wretched woman ; do not terrify your own men .

sections.label: Section 263
sections.original: σιγῶ : σὺν ἄλλοις πείσομαι τὸ μόρσιμον .
sections.translation: I am silent . I will suffer what is destined together with the others .

sections.label: Section 264
sections.original: τοῦτ ἀντ ἐκείνων τοὔπος αἱροῦμαι σέθεν .
sections.translation: I welcome this sentiment of yours over what you said before .

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: καὶ πρός γε τούτοις , ἐκτὸς οὖσ ἀγαλμάτων ,
sections.translation: And in addition , keep your distance from the gods ’ images and make a stronger prayer , that the gods fight on our side . And once you have heard my prayers , then sing the victory song , the sacred cry of joy and goodwill , our Greek ritual of shouting in tribute ,

sections.label: Section 270
sections.translation:
that brings courage to our friends and dissolves fear of the enemy .
And now Here Eteocles makes his vow . to the gods who guard our city ’ s land , both those who dwell in the plain and those who watch over its meeting-place , to Dirce ’ s springs and the waters of Ismenus , I vow that , if things go well and the city is saved ,

sections.label: Section 275
sections.translation: the citizens shall redden the gods ’ altars with the blood of sheep and sacrifice bulls to the gods — this is my vow — and offer trophies , while I will crown their holy temples with the spoil of the enemy ’ s spear-pierced garments .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.translation: Make this kind of prayer to the gods , without your previous lamentation , nor with wild and useless panting ; for you will not escape your destiny any the more . As for me , I will go station six men , with me as the seventh , as champions to oppose the enemy in proud fashion

sections.label: Section 285
sections.translation: at the seven exits in the wall , even before speedy messengers or swift-rushing reports arrive and inflame us with urgent need . Exit .

sections.label: Section 1.288
sections.original: μέλει , φόβῳ δ οὐχ ὑπνώσσει κέαρ :
sections.translation: I heed him , but through terror my heart finds no repose .

sections.label: Section 1.290
sections.translation: Anxieties border upon my heart and kindle my fear of the army surrounding our walls , as a trembling dove fears for her children in the nest because of snakes that are dangerous bed-fellows .

sections.label: Section 1.295
sections.translation: For against our fortifications some are advancing with all their men , all in formation . Ah , what will become of me ? Others are hurling jagged boulders

sections.label: Section 1.300
sections.translation: at the citizens on all sides . O Gods born of Zeus , by every means rescue our city and people , sprung from Cadmus !

sections.label: Section 1.304
sections.translation: What more fertile plain will you find in place of ours ,

sections.label: Section 1.305
sections.translation: if you abandon to the enemy this deep-soiled land and the water of Dirce which is the most nourishing of the streams that earth-encircling Poseidon

sections.label: Section 1.310
sections.translation: and Tethys ’ children pour forth ? Therefore , divine guardians of the city , hurl murderous destruction on the men outside our walls

sections.label: Section 1.315
sections.translation: and panic that makes them throw away their weapons , and so win glory for these citizens . Defend the city and remain in possession of your home and throne

sections.label: Section 1.320
sections.translation: in answer to our shrill , wailing prayers !

sections.label: Section 2.321
sections.translation: It is a great cause for grief to hurl a primeval city to Hades in this way , quarry and slave of the spear , ravaged shamefully in the dusty ashes by an Argive man through divine will .

sections.label: Section 2.325
sections.translation: And grief , too , to let the women be led away captive — ah me !— young and old , dragged by the hair , like horses , with their cloaks torn off them .

sections.label: Section 2.330
sections.translation: A city , emptied , shouts out as the human booty perishes with mingled cries . A heavy fate , indeed , my fear anticipates .

sections.label: Section 2.333
sections.translation: It is a lamentable thing that modest girls should be plucked unripe , before the customary rites , and should make

sections.label: Section 2.335
sections.translation: a loathsome journey from their homes . What ? I declare that the dead will do better than the captives ; for when a city is subdued — ah , ah !— many and miserable are its sufferings .

sections.label: Section 2.340
sections.translation: Man drags off man , or kills , or sets fires ; the whole city is defiled with smoke . Mad Ares storms , subduing the people and polluting reverence .

sections.label: Section 3.345
sections.translation: Tumults swell through the town , and against it a towering net is advancing . Man falls before man beneath the spear . Sobs and wails over gore-covered babes , just nursed at their mothers ’ breasts ,

sections.label: Section 3.350
sections.translation: resound . Rape and pillage of those fleeing through the city are the deeds of one ’ s own blood . Plunderer joins up with plunderer ; the empty-handed calls to the empty-handed , wishing to have a partner ,

sections.label: Section 3.355
sections.translation: each greedy for neither less nor equal share . Reason exists for imagining what will come after this .

sections.label: Section 3.357
sections.original: παντοδαπὸς δὲ καρπὸς χαμάδις πεσὼν ἀλγύνει [ κυρήσας [*] ],
sections.translation: The earth ’ s varied fruits , fallen to the ground , give pain , a bitter sight for the maid-servants .

sections.label: Section 3.360
sections.translation: In jumbled confusion the abundant gifts of earth are carried away by reckless looting waves . Young women , enslaved , suffer a new evil : a bed of misery , prize of the conquering enemy ’ s spear , as though of a prospering husband —

sections.label: Section 3.365
sections.translation: they can expect the coming of the nightly rite , which gives aid to tears and anguish ! [*]

sections.label: Section 369
sections.original: ὅ τοι κατόπτης , ὡς ἐμοὶ δοκεῖ , στρατοῦ
sections.translation: The scout , I believe ,

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: πευθώ τιν ἡμῖν , ὦ φίλαι , νέαν φέρει ,
sections.translation: is bringing some fresh news of the army to us , my friends , since the joints of his legs are hastily speeding as they carry him on his mission .

sections.label: Section 372
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἄναξ ὅδ αὐτὸς Οἰδίπου τόκος
sections.translation: And , indeed , here is our lord himself , the son of Oedipus , at the right moment to hear the messenger ’ s report . Haste makes his stride uneven , too .

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: λέγοιμ ἂν εἰδὼς εὖ τὰ τῶν ἐναντίων ,
sections.translation: It is with certain knowledge that I will give my account of the enemy ’ s actions , how each man according to lot has been posted at the gates . Tydeus is already storming opposite the Proetid gates ; but the seer will not allow him to ford the Ismenus because the omens from the sacrifices are not favorable .

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: Τυδεὺς δὲ μαργῶν καὶ μάχης λελιμμένος [*]
sections.translation: Yet Tydeus , raging and eager for battle , shouts like a serpent hissing at high noon , and lashes skilled Oecles ’ son , with the taunt that he cringes in cowardice before death and battle . With such cries he shakes three overshadowing plumes ,

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: σείει , κράνους χαίτωμ , ὑπ ἀσπίδος δὲ τῷ [*]
sections.translation: his helmet ’ s mane , while from under his shield , bells forged of bronze therein ring out a fearsome clang . He has this haughty symbol on his shield : a well-crafted sky , ablaze with stars , and the brightness of the full moon shining in the center of the shield ,

sections.label: Section 390
sections.translation: the moon that is the most revered of the stars , the eye of night . Raving so in his arrogant armor , he shouts beside the river-bank , craving battle , like some charger that fiercely champs at the bit as he waits in eagerness for the trumpet ’ s war-cry .
